whatif_branches.title: Taiwan Virtual Asset Service Act commencement (subsidiary legislation brings the issuer regime into force)
whatif_branches.trigger_condition: Commencement of the enacted Virtual Asset Service Act through its subsidiary legislation. The Act passed third reading on 30 Jun 2026; commencement awaits FSC subordinate rules and carries no gazetted date.
whatif_branches.basis: Virtual Asset Service Act enacted at third reading 30 Jun 2026; tw-frs-issuer_pathway-001 carries the enacted provisions as status=transitional (binding_status=made_not_commenced), awaiting FSC subsidiary legislation.
whatif_branches.note: This trigger's register-recorded effects move a feasibility signal; the corridors below re-derive accordingly.

whatif_branches.title: US CLARITY Act enacted (digital-asset market structure; intermediary yield + routing layer live)
whatif_branches.trigger_condition: Enactment of the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act (H.R. 3633). On the Senate Legislative Calendar as Calendar No. 423 (reported 1 Jun 2026); still requires Senate floor passage, reconciliation with the Senate Agriculture and House versions, and Presidential signature.
whatif_branches.basis: GENIUS is already in force (the issuer-level layer). CLARITY is the pending market-structure layer (H.R. 3633): us-pss-bank_nonbank_routing-001 binds on it, and the us-pss-permitted_activity_yield-001 / us-pss-securities_classification-001 / us-pss-issuer_pathway-001 cells carry its Sec. 404 and market-structure parts as flagged pending overlays.
whatif_branches.note: The register records this trigger's effects on within-cell fields (binding_status / status / labels), NOT on a feasibility signal — so the register's own rules leave every corridor class unchanged. Shown as-is (conditioning, not forecasting).

whatif_branches.title: Singapore SCS framework implementing legislation enacted (MAS-regulated stablecoin regime live)
whatif_branches.trigger_condition: Enactment of the implementing legislation for the MAS Single-Currency Stablecoin (SCS) framework (finalised 15 Aug 2023). MAS announced at the Nov 2025 Singapore FinTech Festival that it was ready to begin drafting; the framework is expected to take effect in mid-2026.
whatif_branches.basis: The PS Act 2019 DPT baseline is already in force. The SCS-specific framework is finalised MAS policy pending implementing legislation; the sg-scs-* requirement cells carry it as binding_status=finalized_policy_pending, status=transitional.
whatif_branches.note: The register records this trigger's effects on within-cell fields (binding_status / status / labels), NOT on a feasibility signal — so the register's own rules leave every corridor class unchanged. Shown as-is (conditioning, not forecasting).

trigger_kind_legend.purpose: Every event carries a trigger_kind: the paper's typology of regulatory triggers by the kind of certainty each carries (flagship §3). It separates the one kind that moves a dated horizon from the kinds that do not, and names the intra-regime gating change a class-centric reading misses.

kinds.fully-scheduled: gazetted commencement date; applied by that date; the ONLY kind that moves a horizon (§3.1, UK).
kinds.enacted-not-commenced: passed into law but awaiting subsidiary legislation with no gazetted commencement; partly fired (§3.3, TW).
kinds.contingent-no-date: depends on an enactment that has not occurred; no date; surfaced only as an if-enacted branch (§3.2 US, §3.4 KR).
kinds.contingent-not-class-change: a pending enactment that hardens a binding status without moving any class, the baseline class already in force (§3.5, SG).
kinds.parameterization: operationalization of a constraint that already binds; sharpens the operative content of a class without moving one (§3.7, EBA, carried in open_questions).
kinds.intra-regime-gating: a DATED change in law that alters accessibility WITHIN a live regime (who may operate; which tokens are admissible) without moving any feasibility class (§3.10, EU MiCA).
kinds.dated-empty-effect: a dated, now-in-force change with an explicitly empty feasibility-class effect; the control case showing the calendar distinguishes a change in law from a change in feasibility class (§3.6, JP).
kinds.inbound-recognition: a dated, now-in-force change in law that opens an inbound recognition or equivalence channel for qualifying foreign instruments; its reach is set by the recognition criteria and is handled by conditioning (the existing Category II channelled treatment), so it asserts no new class (§3.6 second same-day change; §5.3 dated recognition instance, JP).
kinds.open-compositional: information rather than a change in law, whose resolution feeds another jurisdiction's analysis (§3.8, HK 2nd cohort, carried in open_questions).
kinds.resolved-tightening: an asymmetric posture: a boundary's recent movement is settled in one direction while a reversing development stays open (§3.9, PRC, carried in open_questions).

trigger_kind_legend.horizon_rule: Only fully-scheduled events move a compose() horizon. dated-empty-effect, intra-regime-gating, and inbound-recognition events are dated but move no feasibility class, so they appear on the timeline without changing any corridor's category, by design.

provenance.derivation: Re-runs the register's own compose() (scripts/compose.py; MCP compose_corridor(as_of=...)) over the key dates and over each contingent trigger's if-enacted branch. Every class is a re-derivation of existing tier1_legal signals; no new proposition of law is asserted.
provenance.dated_axis: signals_as_of(date): applies every scheduled/in_force event with effective_date <= date, then re-runs the Atlas algorithm. No probabilities.
provenance.whatif_axis: Each contingent trigger applied in isolation to today's base as an if-enacted branch. Labelled conditioning, never forecasting.
